title: Ageratina corylifolia
taxonomy: Ageratina corylifolia belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it within the broader group of flowering plants, more specifically in the order Asterales. Further classification positions Ageratina corylifolia as a member of the genus Ageratina, which includes other shrubs and small trees native to the Americas. Within the genus Ageratina, Ageratina corylifolia is classified as a species, A. corylifolia. Its classification provides insight into its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the same family and order.
uses: Ageratina corylifolia, also known as Corylifolia, is a plant species used in traditional medicine, particularly in Asian and African cultures. Its leaves, stems, and roots are utilized to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which contribute to its medicinal applications. In addition, Ageratina corylifolia has been used to treat skin problems, such as eczema and dermatitis. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential uses.

side_effects: Ageratina corylifolia is traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions.
